title: rubus mucronulatus
intro: Rubus mucronulatus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan raspberry shrub, is a species of bramble in the rose family (Rosaceae). It is a thorny shrub native to the Himalayas, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Rubus mucronulatus can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a valuable addition to natural health remedies.
